## What's Changed

- Fixed Linux Intel QSV transcoding by initializing FFmpeg with the DRM render device and QSV hardware filter device.
- Added real FFmpeg encoder probes for hardware detection so VAAPI, QSV, NVENC, VideoToolbox, and V4L2 M2M only show as ready when they can actually encode.
- Prefer VAAPI before QSV on Linux Intel systems because it is usually the more reliable container path.
- Added VAAPI/V4L runtime packages to the Docker image and Intel QSV runtime packages on amd64 builds.
- Fixed the CI Docker build so the server binary reports the release version, commit, and timestamp from the tag build.
- Added Tater Tube Player links to the README and corrected the Docker hardware transcoding docs.

## Upgrade

Pull the latest container and restart:

```bash
docker pull ghcr.io/tatertotterson/tater-tube-server:latest
docker restart tater-tube-server
```

For Intel, AMD, Raspberry Pi, and other VAAPI/V4L hardware encoders, pass `/dev/dri` into the container:

```yaml
devices:
  - /dev/dri:/dev/dri
```

For NVIDIA NVENC, keep using the NVIDIA Container Toolkit with `--gpus all`.
